- scope: global
- description: "" (EMPTY)
- skill_level: beginner
- triggers: ["test"]
- required_tools: []
- body: "Test skill body"

Use notes_create with payload wrapper.
I'll create the skill note with the specified parameters using the notes_create function with a payload wrapper.

These fields must be in the 'frontmatter' dict, not at the top level.", "title": "note-lark_notes_create completed", "metadata": {"mcp_server": "note-lark", "mcp_tool": "notes_create"}}]
I see the error - the skill-specific fields need to be in the `frontmatter` dict. Let me fix the structure:

[{"tool": "note-lark_notes_create", "input": {"payload": {"title": "Test Skill", "body": "Test skill body", "frontmatter": {"type": "skill", "scope": "global", "description": "", "skill_level": "beginner", "triggers": ["test"], "required_tools": []}}}, "output": "2 validation errors for NoteCreateRequest
